metadata.title: Guideline on clinical investigation of medicinal products for the treatment of venous thromboembolic disease

rules.rule_text: The primary efficacy outcome in Phase III confirmatory trials for treatment of VTE must be the composite of: documented symptomatic recurrent DVT, documented symptomatic recurrent non-fatal PE, and VTE-related death (non-inferiority trials) or all-cause death (superiority trials).
rules.verbatim_quote: The main efficacy outcome recommended in Phase III trials in the treatment of VTE is the composite of: Documented symptomatic recurrent DVT; Documented symptomatic recurrent non-fatal PE; VTE-related death (non-inferiority trials) or all-cause death (superiority trials).

rules.rule_text: All major endpoints must be adjudicated by a blinded clinical events committee.
rules.verbatim_quote: All major endpoints should be adjudicated by a blinded clinical events committee.

rules.rule_text: Recurrent DVT and PE must be objectively verified. Deaths must be carefully characterised regarding their relationship to VTE according to criteria pre-specified in the study protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: Recurrent DVT and PE should be objectively verified (see section 3). Deaths should be carefully characterised regarding their relationship to VTE, according to criteria to be pre-specified in the study protocol.

rules.rule_text: Diagnosis of symptomatic recurrent DVT or PE based solely on clinical signs and symptoms is discouraged. Objective testing is required.
rules.verbatim_quote: Diagnosis of symptomatic recurrent DVT or PE based solely on clinical signs and symptoms is discouraged.
rules.exceptions: The number of such episodes, especially if leading to changed or renewed therapy, must however be noted and accommodated for in the analyses.

rules.rule_text: The diagnosis of acute recurrent VTE (during the first 3-6 months) must be based on comparison of objective tests with baseline tests performed for initial diagnosis. Recurrences during extended treatment must be compared to a new baseline test performed at end of initial treatment.
rules.verbatim_quote: The diagnosis of "acute" recurrent VTE (i.e. during the first 3-6 months of treatment) should be based on the comparison of objective tests compared to baseline test performed for the initial diagnosis of acute VTE. The diagnosis of recurrences during an extended study after the initial 3 to 6 months of treatment should be compared to a new baseline test (i.e. CUS for VTE or sCT or VPLS for PE) performed at the end of the initial treatment.

rules.rule_text: It is recommended to use the same diagnostic methods for VTE across all trials. The diagnosis of VTE should include clinical probability assessment and D-Dimers to rule out VTE.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is considered that the diagnosis of VTE should be now included in a diagnostic strategy including clinical probability and the use of D-Dimers to rule out VTE. It is recommended to use the same methods for diagnosis of index and recurrent events across all the trial.

rules.rule_text: Clinical trials must focus on patients with symptomatic, usually proximal (above knee level) DVT and/or symptomatic PE. The study population must be stratified by presence of symptomatic PE at baseline and by intended treatment duration.
rules.verbatim_quote: It is expected that clinical trials in patients with acute VTE will focus on patients with symptomatic, usually proximal (extending above knee level) DVT and/or symptomatic PE. Stratification of the study population regarding the presence of symptomatic PE at baseline as well as by intended treatment duration should also be undertaken.

rules.rule_text: Patients with VTE associated to active cancer or central venous catheters, and VTE occurring in children or during pregnancy, require separate studies with appropriate comparators before a claim of use can be granted.
rules.verbatim_quote: Patients with VTE associated to active cancer or central venous catheters (CVC) and VTE occurring in children or during pregnancy have particular characteristics related to clinical presentation, treatment and outcome. Before a claim of use can be granted in these populations, separate studies are needed using an appropriate comparator.

rules.rule_text: For sVT trials, patients must have symptomatic lower limb extensive sVT (at least 5 cm long) confirmed by standardized CUS, with DVT effectively excluded at baseline.
rules.verbatim_quote: in the particular case of clinical trials in the treatment of sVT, it should include patients with symptomatic lower limb extensive (at least 5 cm long) superficial-vein thrombosis, as confirmed by standardized compression ultrasonography (CUS) [9,10]. In sVT, it is crucial for the external validity of the study that DVT is effectively excluded at baseline.

rules.rule_text: All secondary efficacy endpoints must be adjudicated by an independent and blinded committee.
rules.verbatim_quote: All secondary efficacy endpoints should be adjudicated by an independent and blinded committee in order to limit the introduction of any bias.

rules.rule_text: Deaths must be classified using all available methods and categorised as non-vascular, vascular, or unknown aetiology. Vascular deaths must include deaths caused by thromboembolic events, all cardiac deaths, and bleeding.
rules.verbatim_quote: Deaths should be classified using all available methods, including autopsy results, physicians' reports, and other clinical data available. All deaths should preferably be categorised as "non-vascular", "vascular" or "unknown aetiology". Vascular deaths should include deaths caused by thromboembolic events (PE, stroke), all cardiac deaths (e.g.: due to myocardial infarction, heart failure or arrhythmia) and bleeding.

rules.rule_text: A mandatory secondary analysis must include the individual components of the recommended primary efficacy endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: A mandatory secondary analysis should include the individual components of the recommended primary efficacy endpoint.

rules.rule_text: Subgroup analyses are strongly encouraged according to index event (with stratified randomization in trials recruiting both DVT and PE) including: presence/absence of history of previous VTE episodes, level of extension of index and/or recurrent DVT, severity of index and/or recurrent PE.
rules.verbatim_quote: Subgroup analyses are strongly encouraged according to index event (together with stratified randomization in trials recruiting both types of index VTE, DVT and/or PE) and are encouraged to illustrate outcome in relation to: a) presence/absence of a history of previous episodes of VTE; b) level of extension of index and/or recurrent DVT; c) severity of index and/or recurrent PE.

rules.rule_text: For acute VTE treatment, the study period for primary evaluation must be at least 3 months up to 12 months. Controlled data on high-risk patients (idiopathic proximal DVT) treated for at least 6 months must be presented.
rules.verbatim_quote: In studies evaluating medicinal products intended for the acute treatment of VTE, the study period for primary evaluation should be at least three months up to 12 months. ... Unless otherwise justified, controlled data on a sufficient number of patients at high risk for recurrent VTE (idiopathic proximal DVT), treated for at least six months should be presented, with appropriate follow-up of at least 1 month.

rules.rule_text: An appropriate follow-up of at least 1 month after treatment discontinuation must be included to assess a possible rebound effect, regardless of treatment duration.
rules.verbatim_quote: Regardless of treatment duration, an appropriate follow-up of at least 1 month after treatment discontinuation should be included to assess a possible rebound effect.

rules.rule_text: For sVT treatment, the primary endpoint in Phase III trials must be the composite of: documented symptomatic DVT, documented symptomatic non-fatal PE, symptomatic documented extension of sVT, symptomatic documented recurrent sVT, and VTE-related death (non-inferiority) or all-cause death (superiority).
rules.verbatim_quote: In the specific case of Phase III trials in the treatment of sVT, the recommended endpoint is the composite of: Documented symptomatic DVT, Documented symptomatic non-fatal PE, Symptomatic documented extension of sVT, Symptomatic documented recurrent sVT, VTE-related death (non-inferiority trials) or all-cause death (superiority trials).

rules.rule_text: Confirmatory trials must use a prospective, double-blind, randomised, controlled, parallel group design.
rules.verbatim_quote: For confirmatory trials a prospective, double-blind randomised, controlled, parallel group clinical trial is recommended.
rules.exceptions: Open-label studies using VKA as comparator may be acceptable if outcomes are blindly adjudicated, methodology is robust, and results are clinically and statistically meaningful.

rules.rule_text: An active control group is normally required in pivotal studies. For initial VTE treatment, VKA dose-titrated to INR 2.0-3.0 overlapped with parenteral anticoagulant for at least 5 days is an accepted comparator. Direct oral anticoagulants approved in this indication are also valid comparators.
rules.source_section: 7.4.2 Confirmatory trials - Choice of control group
rules.verbatim_quote: An active control group is normally required in pivotal studies due to the severity of the disease to be treated (DVT and/or PE) unless justified. For the initial treatment of VTE, an oral vitamin K antagonist, dose-titrated to an International Normalised Ratio (INR) of 2.0-3.0, overlapped with a parenteral anticoagulant (e.g.: LMWH or fondaparinux) for at least 5 days and until the INR is within therapeutic range, is an accepted comparator. Direct oral anticoagulants approved in this indication are considered valid comparators in this clinical setting as well.
rules.exceptions: Placebo may be appropriate when given on top of standard of care, or for extended prophylaxis of recurrent VTE where clinical equipoise exists regarding continuation or cessation of anticoagulation.

rules.rule_text: For VTE associated with cancer, LMWH (not VKA) must be used as the active comparator.
rules.source_section: 7.4.2 Confirmatory trials - Choice of control group
rules.verbatim_quote: LMWH is currently the drug of choice in VTE associated to cancer [21] and pregnancy [22]. Therefore, LMWH instead of VKA is recommended as active comparator in VTE clinical trials in these situations.

rules.rule_text: When VKA is used as comparator, quality of oral anticoagulation must be measured by TTR using the Rosendaal method. Impact on efficacy and safety must be shown by quartiles of cTTR and by cTTR intervals (<50%, 50-65%, >65%).
rules.source_section: 7.4.2 Confirmatory trials - Quality of oral anticoagulation
rules.verbatim_quote: When VKA is used as comparator, the quality of oral anticoagulation should be based on the time in therapeutic range (TTR) calculated by the Rosendaal method [23]. ... The impact of quality of oral anticoagulation on the main efficacy and safety outcomes has to be shown: By quartiles of centre time in therapeutic range (cTTR)... By cTTR, in the following intervals of cTTR: <50%, 50-65%; >65%.

rules.rule_text: The protocol must describe management of anticoagulant therapy in case of elective and urgent surgical procedures and major trauma.
rules.verbatim_quote: Concomitant procedures: the protocol has to describe the management of anticoagulant therapy during the clinical trial in case of elective and urgent surgical procedures as well as major trauma.

rules.rule_text: The study must include a follow-up of at least 30 days after last day of study drug with a plan for safely transitioning subjects off study medication.
rules.verbatim_quote: The study should include a follow-up of at least 30 days after last day of study drug, and a plan for safely transitioning subjects in case of premature discontinuation of study medication if continued anticoagulation is needed should be properly addressed.

rules.rule_text: Non-inferiority testing (followed by superiority if demonstrated) is the recommended approach in active controlled trials. The non-inferiority margin must be pre-specified and justified.
rules.verbatim_quote: Non-inferiority testing (followed by superiority if non-inferiority is demonstrated) is the recommended approach in active controlled trials. The analysis of non-inferiority and superiority should follow general statistical guidelines (ICH E9). In non-inferiority trials, the choice of the non-inferiority margin should be pre-specified and justified (CPMP/EWP/2158/99).

rules.rule_text: On-treatment analyses and analyses including events occurring 1 week and 1 month after study drug discontinuation must be conducted to investigate possible rebound increase in thromboembolism.
rules.verbatim_quote: 'On-treatment' analyses and analyses including events occurring 1 week and 1 month after study drug discontinuation can be conducted in order to investigate a possible early rebound increase in thromboembolism after treatment cessation.

rules.rule_text: PK profile must be studied in patients with impaired renal function, impaired liver function, and older patients.
rules.verbatim_quote: pharmacokinetic profile of the product in development should also be studied in the following specific patient populations: patients with impaired renal function (EMA/83874/2014), impaired liver function (CPMP/EWP/2339/02), and older patients (CPMP/ICH/379/95).

rules.rule_text: All potential clinically relevant drug-drug or drug-food interactions must be specifically investigated before approval.
rules.verbatim_quote: All potential clinically relevant drug-drug or drug-food interactions derived from the pharmacokinetic or pharmacodynamic characteristics of the investigational drug should be specifically investigated following applicable guidelines (see section 3), preferentially before approval.

rules.rule_text: Patients >65 years and >=75 years must be appropriately represented in clinical trials. Clinical data in older (>=75) and frail oldest older persons (>=85 years) with high comorbidity is of utmost importance.
rules.verbatim_quote: to assess the benefit/risk balance of a drug that will be used in the geriatric population, patients >65 years and >=75 years should be appropriately represented in clinical trials (ICH E7 and Clinical Trials Regulation 536/2014, art 6). ... Generating clinical data in older (>=75) and frail oldest older persons (>=85 years) patients with high comorbidity is a matter of utmost importance.

rules.rule_text: The primary safety endpoint in pivotal trials must be major bleeding. The sum of major and clinically relevant non-major bleeding must be analysed as a secondary endpoint.
rules.verbatim_quote: In pivotal trials, the recommended primary safety endpoint is major bleeding, but the sum of major and clinically relevant non-major bleeding is to be analysed as well (secondary endpoint).

rules.rule_text: Major bleeding is defined as: fatal bleeding; critical organ bleeding (intracranial, intraocular, intraspinal, pericardial, retroperitoneal, intraarticular or intramuscular with compartment syndrome); clinically overt bleeding with haemoglobin decrease >2 g/dL compared with pre-bleeding level; transfusion of >=2 units blood/packed cells; or clinically overt bleeding necessitating surgical intervention.
rules.verbatim_quote: Major bleeding is defined as a bleeding event that meets at least one of the following criteria: fatal bleeding; critical bleeding (intracranial, intraocular, intraspinal, pericardial, retroperitoneal, intraarticular or intramuscular with compartment syndrome); clinically overt bleeding associated with a decrease in the haemoglobin level of more than 2 g/dL (20 g/l; 1.24 mmol/L) compared with the pre-bleeding level; clinically overt bleeding leading to transfusion of two or more units of whole blood or packed cells; clinically overt bleeding that necessitates surgical intervention

rules.rule_text: The adjudication of bleeding events by a central independent and blinded committee of experts is strongly encouraged.
rules.verbatim_quote: Similar to the efficacy evaluation, the adjudication of bleeding events by a central independent and blinded committee of experts, using pre-specified limits and clear terms of reference is strongly encouraged.

rules.rule_text: If there is potential for DILI with the study drugs, an algorithm for hepatic monitoring must be included in the protocol.
rules.verbatim_quote: If there is a potential for drug-induced liver injury (DILI) with the study drugs (experimental and/or control), an algorithm for hepatic monitoring has to be included in the protocol [24]. Available regulatory guidance on DILI should be followed [29].

rules.rule_text: Development of a specific antidote or reversal agent studies and a standardised test for laboratory monitoring of anticoagulant effect are highly recommended.
rules.source_section: 9.1.7 The need for reversal and laboratory monitoring
rules.verbatim_quote: The development of a specific antidote or further specific studies with non-specific reversal agent for new antithrombotics when given at high doses for long-term is highly recommended given the potential for life-threatening bleeding events in standard practice. ... The development of a standardised test for laboratory monitoring of the anticoagulant effect of new agents is highly recommended.

rules.rule_text: Fecal occult blood test (FOBT) at screening and during treatment at regular intervals is encouraged, as long-term antithrombotic therapy may be associated with unperceived chronic GI blood loss.
rules.source_section: 9.1.6 Report and collection of bleeding events and related parameters
rules.verbatim_quote: The use of a fecal occult blood test (FOBT) at screening visit and during treatment at regular intervals is encouraged, since long-term antithrombotic therapy may be associated with unperceived chronic gastrointestinal blood loss.
